events.title: US and Iran Reportedly Close to Peace Deal
events.desc: The United States and Iran have signaled that a deal to end their conflict is imminent, with both sides agreeing on a text. A senior U.S. official stated that Washington expects to sign an initial deal in the coming days, though changes are still possible. This development comes amid ongoing military actions near the Strait of Hormuz and competing narratives from Washington and Tehran regarding the deal's specifics.

events.title: Israel Strikes Lebanon Amid Peace Deal Proximity
events.desc: Despite indications of a potential peace deal between the US and Iran, Israel has continued air strikes in Lebanon. State media reported attacks on cities like Tyre, even after Iran warned Israel to cease hostilities against its ally Hezbollah. This escalation occurs as Iran's foreign minister suggested a deal with the US was nearing.

events.title: US and Iran Exchange Strikes Across Middle East
events.desc: The United States and Iran engaged in retaliatory strikes across the Middle East for a second consecutive day. Iran claimed to have targeted American assets in Kuwait, Jordan, and Bahrain, with Bahrain reporting damage to its capital city overnight. This exchange follows previous US strikes in response to Iran downing a US military helicopter.

events.title: Three Indian Sailors Killed in US Strike on Oil Tanker
events.desc: Three Indian sailors were killed when the US military attacked a Palau-flagged oil tanker. The US stated the strike was in response to the tanker not complying with directions. The crew of the vessel issued a distress call after being hit by a missile fired from a US fighter jet off the coast of Oman.

events.title: US Orders AI Models Disabled for Foreign Nationals
events.desc: The US government has issued an export control directive to AI company Anthropic, ordering it to suspend access to its Fable 5 and Mythos 5 AI models for all foreign nationals. This move could impact international collaboration and the global spread of advanced AI technologies.

events.title: Israeli Strikes Hit Beirut Suburb Days After Truce
events.desc: Israel conducted strikes on a Beirut suburb shortly after a US-brokered truce, citing retaliation for Hezbollah's firing into Israeli territory. The attacks underscore the fragile nature of the ceasefire and the ongoing tensions between Israel and Hezbollah, despite broader efforts towards de-escalation.

events.title: Five Indian Air Force Staff Killed in Transport Plane Crash
events.desc: An Antonov An-32 transport plane belonging to the Indian Air Force crashed near the Jorhat region in Assam. Five air force personnel were killed in the incident, highlighting potential safety concerns within military aviation.

events.title: Pakistan PM Claims Final US-Iran Peace Deal Text Agreed
events.desc: Pakistan's Prime Minister Shehbaz Sharif announced that a final, agreed text for a peace deal between the United States and Iran has been reached. Islamabad is reportedly working with both parties to finalize the subsequent steps, with an electronic signing anticipated within 24 hours.

events.title: Satellite Imagery Shows Ongoing Demolitions in Southern Lebanon
events.desc: Recent satellite imagery reveals extensive damage and demolition in southern Lebanon, with at least 46 out of 54 towns and villages within the Israeli defense force's 'Yellow Line' heavily impacted. Much of this destruction appears to have occurred recently, despite a fragile ceasefire.

events.title: Israel Attacks Lebanon Despite Peace Deal Proximity
events.desc: Israel has continued attacks on Lebanon, even as Iran indicated it was included in a potential peace deal. This ongoing kinetic action complicates diplomatic efforts and suggests a persistent conflict dynamic despite high-level negotiations.

events.title: US Strikes Iran in Response to Helicopter Downing
events.desc: President Donald Trump vowed retaliation after accusing Iran of shooting down a US military helicopter over the Strait of Hormuz. The subsequent US strikes on Iran are a significant escalation, directly targeting Iranian assets and signaling a heightened risk of further conflict.

events.title: Banned Russian Submunitions Found in Mali
events.desc: Unexploded Russian-made cluster munition bomblets have been discovered in northern Mali, a nation that is a state party to the relevant convention. This finding follows Mali's military announcement of airstrikes, raising concerns about the use of banned weapons and potential violations of international law.

events.title: Russia Expands Grain Smuggling to Libya
events.desc: A new technique using AIS data has helped track the expansion of Russian grain smuggling operations from occupied Ukrainian ports to Libya. The bulk carrier Grumant was observed loading grain in Feodosia, Crimea, before embarking on a journey to Benghazi, Libya, indicating a continued pattern of illicit trade.

events.title: Israeli Strikes in Southern Lebanon Kill 17
events.desc: Reports indicate that Israeli air strikes in southern Lebanon have resulted in the deaths of 17 individuals. Nine fatalities occurred in a series of attacks in the town of Tayr Debba, according to Lebanese state news agency. This incident highlights the ongoing kinetic exchanges in the region.

events.title: US-Iran War Deal Looms Amid New Military Action
events.desc: As the United States and Iran signal a peace deal is close, new military actions are flaring near the Strait of Hormuz. This juxtaposition highlights the complex and volatile security environment, where diplomatic progress is being made amidst continued kinetic exchanges.
